title: Schedonorus pauciflorus
intro: Schedonorus pauciflorus, also known as timothy grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. The health benefits of Schedonorus pauciflorus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of asthma, allergies, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Timothy grass is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and supplements, often in the form of timothy grass pollen extract, which is used to support respiratory health and immune function.
taxonomy: Schedonorus pauciflorus is a species of grass, and it belongs to the family Poaceae. This classification makes it a member of the monocotyledonous flowering plants. The genus Schedonorus is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which includes many species of grasses. Schedonorus pauciflorus is further classified as a type of bunchgrass, characterized by its ability to grow in dense clusters. Its classification as a grass places it within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Schedonorus pauciflorus, also known as Poa pauciflora, is a species of grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous peoples of the region.
